title: Password Protect PDF — Secure Files Offline
h1: Add Password Protection to PDF Files Instantly
description: Encrypt PDF files with a strong password directly in your browser. Your document never leaves your device. Ideal for users in Oslo.

sections.title: Why Protect PDFs Locally?
sections.content: Adding a password means the file is sensitive. Never upload sensitive files to a cloud encryptor. Use ours — it runs entirely offline.

sections.title: How to Password Protect a PDF

steps.title: Open PDF
steps.description: Select the PDF you want to lock.

steps.title: Set Password
steps.description: Choose a strong password for encryption.

steps.title: Download Protected PDF
steps.description: Get your AES-256 encrypted PDF immediately.

sections.title: Secure PDFs for Oslo Professionals
sections.content: Legal and finance professionals in Oslo use our tool to password-protect sensitive documents offline.

sections.title: AES-256 Encryption Locally
sections.content: Bank-grade AES-256 encryption applied directly in your browser with zero server involvement.

sections.title: Encrypt Any PDF in Under 1 Second
sections.content: Applying AES-256 encryption locally takes milliseconds — no upload, no queue.

faqs.a: AES-256, the same standard used by banks.

faqs.q: Can I set different owner and user passwords?
faqs.a: Yes, you can set both separately.
No, a new encrypted copy is created.

faqs.q: Can I restrict printing or editing?
faqs.a: Yes, permission restrictions can be set alongside the password.
